content_profile.learning_objective: Explain how NeRF queries a continuous neural radiance field along camera rays and integrates density, color, and transmittance into a rendered pixel.
content_profile.visual_thesis: A camera ray becomes an ordered sequence of sample points; each point queries a continuous field for density and color, and ordered volume integration turns those contributions into a pixel.
scope: Show one camera pixel casting one ray through a 3D volume.
Show ordered samples along that ray.
Show F_Theta(x,d) returning density and view-dependent color.
Show transmittance-weighted color accumulation into one output pixel, then hint at sweeping many rays.

entities.label: r(t)
entities.technical_role: Ray passing from a camera pixel through the 3D scene volume.
entities.meaning: Defines the ordered path along which the field is sampled.
entities.claim_boundary: Single ray explains one pixel, not a full rendered image.

entities.label: x = r(t)
entities.technical_role: 3D positions sampled along the camera ray.
entities.meaning: Locations where the neural field is queried.
entities.visualization_need: Must be ordered along the ray.
entities.claim_boundary: Sample spacing is pedagogical unless a sampling scheme is specified.

entities.label: d
entities.technical_role: Viewing direction input to the radiance field.
entities.meaning: Allows emitted color to be view-dependent.
entities.visualization_need: Should travel with field queries as a direction cue.
entities.claim_boundary: One fixed viewing direction is shown for the selected ray.

entities.label: F_Theta
entities.technical_role: Neural radiance field mapping position and direction to density and color.
entities.meaning: A continuous function queried at sample points, not an explicit voxel table.
entities.claim_boundary: Rendered as a module to avoid implying stored voxels.

entities.label: sigma
entities.technical_role: Volume density output at a sample point.
entities.meaning: Controls opacity and contribution probability along the ray.
entities.claim_boundary: Values are qualitative without a toy table.

entities.label: c
entities.technical_role: View-dependent color output at a sample point.
entities.meaning: Color contribution that may enter the rendered pixel.
entities.visualization_need: Needs visible color chips tied to sample positions.

entities.label: T(t)
entities.technical_role: Accumulated visibility along the ray before a sample.
entities.meaning: Earlier density can reduce later samples' visibility.
entities.visualization_need: Requires near-to-far fading along the ray.
entities.claim_boundary: Ribbon fade is qualitative unless toy densities are supplied.

entities.label: C(r)
entities.technical_role: Final color for the camera ray after volume rendering.
entities.meaning: Weighted accumulation of colors and densities along the ray.
entities.visualization_need: Needs a payoff target for accumulated color chips.
entities.claim_boundary: One pixel is a local explanation before sweeping many rays.

operators.label: Cast camera ray
operators.technical_meaning: Project one camera pixel into a ray through the 3D scene volume.
operators.visual_operation_hint: A yellow ray leaves the image plane and cuts through the volume.

operators.label: Sample along ray
operators.technical_meaning: Choose ordered 3D locations along the ray.
operators.visual_operation_hint: Blue beads appear in near-to-far order along the ray.

operators.label: Query radiance field
operators.technical_meaning: Evaluate F_Theta at each sample position and viewing direction to obtain density and color.
operators.visual_operation_hint: Each sample sends a query to the field module and receives opacity plus color.

operators.label: Volume integration
operators.technical_meaning: Integrate color contributions weighted by density and accumulated transmittance along the ray.
operators.visual_operation_hint: Color chips stack into the output pixel while the visibility ribbon fades with depth.

states.label: Ray ready
states.description: One camera ray passes through the 3D volume from near to far.

states.label: Samples ready
states.description: Ordered sample points are placed along the ray.

states.label: Field outputs ready
states.description: Each sample has a density and emitted color output from the field.

states.label: Pixel accumulated
states.description: Transmittance-weighted color contributions have accumulated into one pixel.

relations.meaning: Sample positions lie along the selected ray.
The field maps query inputs to density and color outputs.
Accumulated transmittance modulates how much later samples affect the final pixel.

invariants.statement: NeRF is represented as a continuous queried field, not an explicit voxel grid.
invariants.visual_test: The field is shown as a function module queried by beads, not as a fixed voxel table.

invariants.statement: Samples retain near-to-far order along the camera ray.
invariants.visual_test: Sample IDs and transmittance fade move monotonically along ray depth.

invariants.statement: Sample contributions are modulated by density and transmittance, not all weighted equally.
invariants.visual_test: Color chip opacity or thickness changes with density and visibility.

assumptions.statement: The main explanation follows one ray to explain one pixel before hinting at many rays.
assumptions.impact_on_visualization: Avoid implying a single ray explains the whole image.

assumptions.statement: Sample values, densities, and colors are schematic unless a toy table is supplied.
assumptions.impact_on_visualization: Do not display exact numeric weights without explicit toy values.

spaces.coordinate_system: camera image plane plus 3D scene volume
spaces.purpose: Show ray geometry and sample ordering in the scene volume.
spaces.depth_semantics: Depth is ray depth from near to far.

spaces.coordinate_system: sample query inputs to field outputs
spaces.purpose: Show F_Theta(x,d) returning sigma and c.
Keep volume rendering and transmittance formulas readable.

objects.visual_form: camera image plane with one highlighted pixel
objects.renderer_notes: Keep the selected pixel visible as the ray origin.
Do not reverse near and far order.

objects.visual_form: ordered blue beads along the ray
objects.renderer_notes: Spacing may be schematic, but order must be exact.

objects.visual_form: green F_Theta module receiving x and d
objects.renderer_notes: Module label must be deterministic and should not look like a voxel grid.

objects.visual_form: color chips paired with sample beads
objects.renderer_notes: Color chips can be illustrative but must keep sample association.

objects.visual_form: red visibility ribbon fading from near to far
objects.renderer_notes: Ribbon fade represents accumulated transmittance qualitatively.
Show as one pixel before sweeping to many rays.

objects.visual_form: F_Theta(x,d)->(c,sigma), T(t), and C(r) integral formulas

encodings.risk_note: Density, color, and transmittance encodings must remain distinct.
Opacity means density-weighted contribution, not confidence.
Depth order must not be reversed by camera motion.

layout_constraints.rule: Sample beads keep near-to-far order along the ray throughout integration.
Hold the formula panel and F_Theta label still during field-query explanations.
Do not arrange samples as a voxel table; show them as queries into a continuous field.

beats.teaching_goal: Show one camera pixel casting one ray through the scene volume.
beats.viewer_question: What does one output pixel query in the 3D scene?

beats.teaching_goal: Show ordered sample points along the ray.
beats.viewer_question: Where does NeRF evaluate the field?

beats.teaching_goal: Show each sample and view direction querying F_Theta for density and color.
beats.viewer_question: What does the neural field return at each sample?

beats.teaching_goal: Show transmittance-weighted accumulation into the rendered pixel.
beats.viewer_question: How do sample outputs become one pixel color?

object_states.state: ray traced from camera through volume
ordered sample beads appear along the ray
F_Theta receives sample positions and viewing direction
density opacity beads attached to samples
visibility ribbon fades from near to far
color contributions accumulated into final pixel

transitions.technical_event: A camera pixel defines a ray through the scene volume.

object_correspondence.correspondence_note: The highlighted pixel is the origin of the selected ray.

transitions.timing_note: Highlight pixel first, then trace ray.

transitions.technical_event: The ray is sampled at ordered 3D positions.

object_correspondence.correspondence_note: Every bead lies on the selected ray.

transitions.timing_note: Place samples from near to far.

transitions.technical_event: Each sample and view direction query F_Theta for sigma and c.

object_correspondence.correspondence_note: Each density bead remains attached to its originating sample.
Each color chip remains attached to its originating sample.

transitions.timing_note: Query a few samples explicitly, then reveal outputs for the rest.

transitions.technical_event: Density, color, and transmittance are integrated along the ray into C(r).

object_correspondence.correspondence_note: Weighted color chips contribute to the final pixel.

transitions.timing_note: Fade transmittance ribbon before moving weighted colors into the pixel.

motion_primitives.technical_meaning: Trace the ray corresponding to one camera pixel.
motion_primitives.misread_risk: The ray should not be confused with a full image render.

motion_primitives.technical_meaning: Place sample points along the camera ray.
motion_primitives.misread_risk: Sample positions should not look like a voxel grid.

motion_primitives.technical_meaning: Keep sample beads ordered by ray depth.
motion_primitives.misread_risk: Camera orbit can visually invert order if labels are absent.

motion_primitives.technical_meaning: Send sample position and view direction into F_Theta.
motion_primitives.misread_risk: The field module must not be drawn as stored voxels.

motion_primitives.technical_meaning: Reveal sigma and c outputs for queried samples.
motion_primitives.misread_risk: Density and color must be visually distinct.

motion_primitives.technical_meaning: Show accumulated transmittance decreasing along the ray.
motion_primitives.misread_risk: Fading should mean visibility, not sample deletion.

motion_primitives.technical_meaning: Accumulate weighted color contributions into the output pixel.
motion_primitives.misread_risk: Do not make every color chip contribute equally.

complexity_budget.notes: Keep one selected ray through all beats; only hint at many-ray sweep after the one-pixel mechanism is clear.

shots.hold_reason: Hold after establishing the 3D camera-ray relationship.
shots.depth_usage: Depth represents ray traversal through the 3D scene volume.

shots.hold_reason: Hold on the integral before aggregating chips into the pixel.

camera_intents.intent: Use 3D camera movement only to clarify ray depth and sample ordering.
Hold camera during F_Theta, T(t), and C(r) formulas.

readability_constraints: Do not draw a voxel grid.
Use deterministic rendering for formulas and F_Theta labels.
State that one ray explains one pixel before hinting at many rays.

exact_required.reason: Field query and volume rendering formulas are source anchors.
Ray direction and near-to-far ordering must be preserved.
Sample order along the ray is part of the mechanism.
F_Theta must be shown as a continuous queried field.

metaphor_allowed.allowed_scope: 3D ray, sample beads, density opacity, and color chips can make ray integration inspectable.
metaphor_allowed.boundary: They are not a voxel grid or exact sample values.

ai_video_forbidden: F_Theta, sigma, c, T(t), C(r) labels
Voxel-grid depiction as the main representation
Claims about training or hierarchical sampling
Fake numeric density or transmittance values

simplifications.statement: The example focuses on one ray and one output pixel.
simplifications.impact: It teaches the local rendering mechanism before generalizing to many rays.
simplifications.disclosure_or_guardrail: Narration should say a full image repeats this for many rays.

simplifications.statement: Density, color, and transmittance values are schematic.
simplifications.impact: Opacity and color show dependency structure, not real model outputs.
simplifications.disclosure_or_guardrail: Avoid numeric labels unless a toy table is introduced.

responsibilities: Render the 3D camera, ray, scene volume, ordered sample beads, and color accumulation.
Preserve near-to-far sample ordering during camera movement.
candidate_renderers.reason: NeRF requires inspectable 3D geometry rather than a flat diagram alone.
risks: Camera motion can hide labels or invert perceived order if uncontrolled.

responsibilities: Render formulas, labels, field-query diagrams, and exact overlays.
Provide a fallback 2.5D ray diagram if Three.js is unavailable.
candidate_renderers.reason: Readable math and labels must be deterministic.
risks: Standalone 2D rendering may under-explain ray depth.

responsibilities: Optional polished 3D ray and volume scene after the exact layout is validated.
candidate_renderers.reason: Useful for final cinematic polish when exact overlays remain separate.
risks: Can become decorative if not constrained by the ray-order contract.

responsibilities: No formulas, labels, ray topology, or sample ordering.
candidate_renderers.reason: AI video cannot be trusted with NeRF math or exact ray topology.
risks: May invent voxel grids or fake labels.

fallbacks.tradeoff: Use a 2.5D ray diagram with explicit depth labels instead of an inspectable 3D scene.
Keep web-native 3D scene without final cinematic polish.

storyboard_beat_refs.storyboard_note: Highlight one pixel and trace its ray through a translucent scene volume.
Place sample beads near-to-far along the ray.
Route sample positions and viewing direction into F_Theta and reveal sigma plus c.
Fade transmittance and aggregate weighted colors into one output pixel.

enriched_scene_spec_hints.notes: Establish that this is one pixel and one ray.
Show continuous field query, not voxel lookup.
Make density/transmittance modulation visible before final pixel payoff.

downstream_tasks: Write storyboard.md for ray, samples, field query, and integration beats.
Create a toy sample table only if numeric labels are desired.
Generate a Three.js prototype for ray-order QA.

source_fidelity_checks: F_Theta is shown as a continuous queried field, not an explicit voxel grid.
Volume rendering uses density, emitted color, and transmittance.
The single-ray explanation is framed as one pixel before generalizing.
visual_readability_checks: Near-to-far sample order remains visible under camera motion.
F_Theta, sigma, c, T(t), and C(r) labels are deterministic and readable.
Color chips remain attached to their sample origins until aggregation.
misconception_checks: The scene does not imply all samples contribute equally.
The field is not drawn as a voxel grid.
The single-ray walkthrough is not presented as the whole image.
renderer_acceptance_checks: Exact formulas are rendered by Manim or equivalent deterministic overlays.
3D ray scene preserves topology under camera movement.
AI video providers are not used for labels, formulas, ray topology, or sample ordering.

open_questions.question: Should this example include a toy density/color/transmittance table for numeric exactness?

known_risks.risk: Viewers may assume NeRF stores a voxel grid if the volume is drawn too discretely.
known_risks.mitigation: Show sample beads as queries into F_Theta and avoid grid-like volume cells.
